trade.summary: Saudi-Turkey trade is significant and growing. Saudi Arabia exports crude oil and petrochemicals to Turkey. Turkey exports food products, machinery, textiles, chemicals, and construction services to Saudi Arabia. Turkish construction companies have been major contractors for Saudi Vision 2030 projects. Turkish food exports (including Halal-certified products) are valued in Saudi Arabia. Post-normalization trade is growing rapidly.

diplomatic.status: normalizing after serious rupture — Khashoggi affair relations restored 2022
diplomatic.summary: Saudi-Turkey relations experienced a severe crisis following the 2018 murder of Saudi journalist Jamal Khashoggi at the Saudi consulate in Istanbul. Turkey pursued the investigation aggressively and publicly, directly implicating Crown Prince MBS. The rupture lasted until 2022, when Erdogan visited Riyadh and pursued normalization as Turkey faced an economic crisis and needed Gulf investment. Both countries have competing visions for Sunni Muslim leadership and Middle East order (Turkey supports Muslim Brotherhood-aligned actors; Saudi Arabia opposes them). Relations are now pragmatic but carry underlying strategic competition.
recent_incidents: Khashoggi murder in Saudi consulate Istanbul (2018) — severe bilateral crisis
Turkey transferred Khashoggi trial to Saudi Arabia (2022) — criticized by rights groups but facilitated normalization
Erdogan visited Riyadh April 2022 — normalization summit
Saudi Arabia made significant investments in Turkey post-normalization
Competing positions on Muslim Brotherhood, Hamas, Qatar blockade

military.summary: Both are significant regional military powers with competing interests. Turkey supported Qatar during Saudi-led blockade (2017-2021). Turkey and Saudi Arabia have opposing positions on Libya and Syria. Turkey's support for the Muslim Brotherhood conflicts with Saudi interests. Some arms trade potential given Turkey's growing defence export industry. Qatar (Turkish ally) and Saudi Arabia have had tense relations — Turkey positioned as a check on Saudi regional dominance.
military.alliance_status: none — regional competitors with some coordination
incidents: Turkey's Qatar military base stance directly countered Saudi interests during 2017 blockade

financial.summary: Post-normalization, Saudi Arabia has made significant financial investments in Turkey — helping stabilize the Turkish lira during 2022 financial crisis. Saudi Arabia's PIF invested in Turkish equities and deposited funds in Turkey's central bank. Saudi Aramco has discussions on Turkey's energy sector. Turkish construction companies have major contracts in Saudi Arabia's Vision 2030 projects.

energy.summary: Saudi Arabia supplies crude oil to Turkey. Turkey is interested in Saudi gas supplies through alternative routes. Both countries are interested in the broader energy geopolitics of the Middle East and Caspian region. Saudi Arabia's transition investments may include Turkish renewable energy companies.

scientific.summary: Growing scientific collaboration post-normalization. Turkish and Saudi universities have exchange programs. KAUST and Turkish universities cooperate on energy technology and materials science. Turkey's growing research sector makes it an attractive partner for Saudi academic outreach.
